text.title: Microservices Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Microservices Architecture` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each service maps to ONE DDD bounded context. Services spanning > 1 context = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Cross-context services replicate monolith pain at higher distributed cost.

rule.statement: Each service owns its own DB schema; cross-service data access goes through the owning service's API.
rule.rationale: Shared schema = invisible coupling + lockstep deploys.

rule.statement: Service API changes MUST be backwards-compatible OR ship as a versioned endpoint with a deprecation window.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes between independently-deployed services trigger lockstep outages.

rule.statement: Consumer-driven contract tests (Pact / Spring Cloud Contract) MUST gate every service change.
rule.rationale: Integration tests scale poorly across N services.

rule.statement: Strangler-Fig extractions migrate ONE bounded context at a time; big-bang rewrites are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Big-bang rewrites fail at higher rates than gradual extraction.

rule.statement: Decomposition requires a stated trigger — team size, scaling asymmetry, deploy-lockstep pain. 'Modernity' is not a trigger.
rule.rationale: Premature decomposition multiplies cost without value.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
